name: Agent Context Protection Policy
description: Mandatory operational safety rules for all agents interacting with external UIs, browser automation, credential handling, and context-window management. Protection-first mindset.

rules.title: Never accumulate binary assets in conversation context
rules.description: Screenshots, images, and large base64 payloads must NOT be repeatedly loaded into agent conversation context. Each message re-transmits the full history — accumulated images will exceed API size limits and cause unrecoverable 400 errors ('Could not process image'). The conversation becomes permanently broken.
mitigation: Save screenshots to disk (/tmp/ or data/) — do NOT call view_image on every navigation step
Describe screenshot contents in text instead of embedding the image
If an image must be viewed, view it ONCE, summarize findings, then never reference it again
Keep total image views per conversation under 10
Use small viewport sizes (1280x800 max) to reduce image payload size

rules.title: Credential isolation — zero trust by default
rules.description: Agent conversations are logged, cached, and potentially transmitted to remote APIs. Credentials must NEVER appear in agent context, tool parameters, or logs.
mitigation: Use noVNC visual login: agent navigates to login page, human types credentials in the browser window
Store secrets in SecretsVault (AES-256-GCM) via backend/gateway/secrets.py
Reference secrets by vault key name, never by value
If an agent needs a credential at runtime, it reads from vault — the value is never echoed or logged
Router admin, GoDaddy, Doppler, and all service passwords are vault-only

rules.title: Browser session timeout awareness
rules.description: Router admin UIs, cloud dashboards, and authenticated web apps have session timeouts (typically 5 minutes). Agents must work fast and save frequently.
mitigation: After every significant config change, click Save/Apply immediately
Do not leave authenticated sessions idle while performing other work
If a session times out, request human re-login via noVNC — do NOT attempt to auto-login
Batch related UI changes together to minimize session timeout risk

rules.title: Network device safety — reserved ports and configs
rules.description: Some network configurations are explicitly protected. Agents must check the reserved_do_not_touch list before modifying any port, VLAN, or firewall rule.
mitigation: Xbox Live ports (3074, 88, 500, 3544, 4500) are NEVER modified
Check network_vlan_strategy.json reserved_do_not_touch before any firewall/port change
WAN port configuration requires explicit human approval
Port forwarding rules are append-only — never delete existing rules without human confirmation

rules.title: Context window preservation
rules.description: Long-running multi-step tasks accumulate context. When context grows too large, the conversation degrades or fails. Agents must actively manage context budget.
mitigation: For multi-step UI automation, summarize progress in memory files rather than keeping full history in context
Use session memory (/memories/session/) to checkpoint progress so new conversations can resume
When a conversation hits 15+ tool calls, consider checkpointing to memory and suggesting a fresh chat
Store structured state (VLAN configs, device IPs, task progress) in data/ files, not conversation history

rules.title: Browser-worker isolation
rules.description: The browser-worker pod runs inside Kubernetes for isolation. Agents must not bypass this boundary.
mitigation: All browser automation goes through the browser-worker REST API (localhost:8082)
BROWSER_ALLOWED_PREFIXES restricts navigation to LAN IPs only by default
To access external sites (GoDaddy, etc.), allowlist must be explicitly expanded with human approval
Browser-worker has no access to the host filesystem or other pods

incident_log.incident: Conversation permanently broken by accumulated router screenshots
incident_log.cause: Agent called view_image on every router navigation step (~20+ screenshots). API returned 400 'Could not process image' on all subsequent messages.
incident_log.resolution: Started new chat. Created ACP-1 rule to prevent recurrence.
